title: Kubernetes v1.36: Fine-Grained Kubelet API Authorization Graduates to GA
description:
On behalf of Kubernetes SIG Auth and SIG Node, we are pleased to announce the
graduation of fine-grained kubelet API authorization to General Availability
(GA) in Kubernetes v1.36!
The KubeletFineGrainedAuthz feature gate was introduced as an opt-in

title: Kubernetes v1.36: User Namespaces in Kubernetes are finally GA
description:
After several years of development, User Namespaces support in
Kubernetes reached General Availability (GA) with the v1.36 release.
This is a Linux-only feature.
For those of us working on low level container runtimes and rootless
technologies, this

title: SELinux Volume Label Changes goes GA (and likely implications in v1.37)
description:
If you run Kubernetes on Linux with SELinux in enforcing mode, plan ahead: a future release (anticipated to be v1.37) is
expected to turn the SELinuxMount feature gate on by default. This makes volume setup faster
for most workloads, but it can break

description:

title: Gateway API v1.5: Moving features to Stable
description:
The Kubernetes SIG Network community presents the release of Gateway API (v1.5)!
Released on February 27, 2026, version 1.5 is our biggest release yet, and concentrates on moving existing Experimental features to Standard (Stable).

title: Kubernetes v1.36 Sneak Peek
description:
Kubernetes v1.36 is coming at the end of April 2026. This release will include removals and deprecations, and it is packed with an impressive number of
enhancements. Here are some of the features we are most excited about in this cycle!

title: Announcing Ingress2Gateway 1.0: Your Path to Gateway API
description:
With the Ingress-NGINX retirement scheduled for March 2026, the Kubernetes networking landscape is at a turning point.
For most organizations, the question isn't whether to migrate to Gateway API, but how to do so safely.

title: The Invisible Rewrite: Modernizing the Kubernetes Image Promoter
description:

title: Before You Migrate: Five Surprising Ingress-NGINX Behaviors You Need to Know
description:
As announced November 2025, Kubernetes will retire Ingress-NGINX in March 2026.
Despite its widespread usage, Ingress-NGINX is full of surprising defaults and side effects that are probably present in your cluster today.

title: Kubernetes v1.36: New Metric for Route Sync in the Cloud Controller Manager
description:
Kubernetes v1.36 introduces a new alpha counter metric route_controller_route_sync_total
to the Cloud Controller Manager (CCM) route controller implementation at
k8s.io/cloud-provider. This metric
increments each time routes are synced with the cloud

title: Experimenting with Gateway API using kind
description:

title: Cluster API v1.12: Introducing In-place Updates and Chained Upgrades
description:
Cluster API brings declarative management to Kubernetes cluster lifecycle, allowing users and platform teams to define the desired state of clusters and rely on controllers to continuously reconcile toward it.

title: Headlamp in 2025: Project Highlights
